data.title: Eliminate octahedron face-texture flash on home-screen render (preload + fade-in)

data.body:
REVIEW (agent:claude-opus-4-reviewer) — VERDICT: PASS

Reviewed commit 95b9396 cold against plan acceptance criteria. Ran typecheck (pass), build (pass), hero-scroll tests (11/11 pass), full suite (7 pre-existing failures in buttons/footer/navigation/neighborhood — none reference FRAC-192 code; commit touches zero test files, so these are confirmed unrelated).

VERIFIED STATICALLY/BY BUILD:
1. Two-shell cross-dissolve z-orders correctly. Opaque backdrop (radius 1, depthWrite on) renders first; textured shell (radius 1.001, transparent, depthWrite off during fade) is physically closer to camera on every front face, so depthTest passes and it draws over the backdrop with no z-fighting (0.001 offset). Both shells FrontSide, so back faces are culled — no rear-face bleed. Correct.
2. Per-face fade not reset by sibling arrivals: textured materials live in a useRef array (texturedFacesRef), created exactly once in the loader .then and slot-swapped imperatively. forceRender re-derives texturedMatArray but never rebuilds the in-flight material objects, so opacity (mutated by useFrame) persists. The old useMemo-rebuild-all bug is fixed. Correct.
3. Reduced motion (FRAC-28): useFrame snaps mat.opacity=1 immediately when prefersReducedMotion, then flips transparent=false/depthWrite=true on the same frame — instant show, no animation. Scale lock also preserved (moved to groupRef so both shells + hit target scale together; reduced-motion setScalar(0.9) and hover 0.9->0.95 lerp intact). Correct.
4. Graceful failure: empty textured slot has colorWrite=false, opacity=0, transparent=true, depthWrite=false — fully inert, draws nothing, backdrop solid color shows through permanently on 404. Never transparent/invisible face. Correct.
5. FRAC-35 sRGB (tex.colorSpace=SRGBColorSpace) and FRAC-41 (plain map, no tint/overlay) preserved in the new textured material path.
6. Disposal on unmount: textured materials + textures + transparent placeholders disposed in cleanup. NOTE: successfully-loaded materials are disposed twice (once via created[], once via texturedFacesRef slots) — harmless in three.js (dispose() just dispatches an event; renderer releases GPU resource idempotently), but slightly redundant. Not a defect.
7. Preload href correctness: all 8 banner hrefs match FACE_BANNER_IMAGES byte-for-byte (case, leading slash, no crossorigin) -> warm-cache hit, no double fetch. AVIF preload imagesrcset/imagesizes (640/1280/1920/2560w, 100vw) mirrors Hero.tsx AVIF <source> exactly; AVIF source is first so type-match is correct.
8. FRAC-181 intact: dist/_headers script preloads are ONLY react-vendor + vite-preload-helper — NOT three-vendor or FractalCityScene. index.html adds as=image hints only.
9. Payload: all 8 webps confirmed 512x512 (sips), total 114946 bytes (~112KB) vs ~437KB originals (~74% reduction) — matches claim. dist/ serves the 512² banners. compress-banners.mjs SOURCE_TO_OUTPUT correctly maps school->new-liberal-arts.webp and forum->political-club.webp; build:banners wired in package.json.

COULD NOT VERIFY (requires throttled browser — out of scope for static review): the actual visual smoothness of the dissolve under Fast-4G+4x-CPU at 375px/1440px, and that Network-panel initiator shows the preload <link> (not the JS chunk) firing the fetch. The code structure strongly supports both, but the live throttled-network visual flash test (AC #1, #2 initiator) remains browser-confirmation pending. Recommend a quick loopy browser check before/after merge.

Mobile-first (PRD): change is viewport-agnostic 3D rendering; banner downscale and preloads benefit mobile (smaller payload, earlier fetch). No mobile regression. Aligns with PRD.
